hypothesis: A breakout above (or below) the Bollinger band combined with strong momentum signals from RSI and Stochastic reduces false entries in a volatile crypto pair; using ATR for dynamic stops and targets adapts risk to current market conditions.

Combining a Bollinger band breakout with a strong RSI confirmation and a trend filter that requires the price to stay above the middle band reduces false breakouts in ranging markets, while ATR‑based SL/TP adapts to volatility to limit drawdown.

A price breakout beyond the Bollinger upper/lower bands is more likely to sustain a trend when confirmed by both RSI and Stochastic oscillators being in extreme overbought/oversold zones; this triple confirmation filters out false breakouts in ranging markets and aligns entry with momentum, while exit is triggered when any of the three indicators move back toward neutrality, providing a robust risk‑to‑reward profile.

The combination of a price breakout beyond the Bollinger upper/lower bands, an RSI extreme (>70 or <30), and a stochastic extreme (>80 or <20) provides a triple confirmation of momentum and trend strength, while ATR-based risk limits adapt to volatility, reducing false entries and protecting against sharp reversals.
